title: "Mounted volumes unavailable afer upgrade to fesity"

forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades

### Post by higgins on 2007-04-24
Hi there

I previously had two volumes (mounting automatically at login) before the upgrade to feisty, /media/data (this is a SATA partition called MACDATA) AND /media/dvd, now they do not mount when I log in..

However the following now mounts automatically on startup: /media/MACDATA

Here is my fstab:

```
tim@tim-ubuntu:/etc$ cat fstab
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/mapper/sda1 :
UUID=e8e4d520-fc21-4121-9c5d-8c429bcc8fa8 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/mapper/sda5 :
UUID=c8f48285-df24-4310-aca7-eab8ffe59ec4 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/cdrom /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sda5 /media/data vfat iocharset=utf8,umask=000 0 0
/dev/sda2 /media/dvd vfat iocharset=utf8,umask=000 0 0

```

How do I correct this? I checked the Ubuntu 7.04 guide but it seems to be out of date. (It refers to hd[x] etc, from what I understand its now sda???)

Actaully, looking in My Computer there are three of my drives visible:

1.JasInstal (HFS+)
2. MACDATA (FAT32)
3. FileSystem (Ubuntu FileSystem)

However when checking /media I have:

```
tim@tim-ubuntu:/media$ ls -l
total 44
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root     6 2007-03-17 16:19 cdrom -> cdrom0
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root  4096 2007-03-17 16:19 cdrom0
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root  4096 2007-03-17 23:04 data
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root  4096 2007-03-24 22:52 dvd
drwx------ 8 tim  root 32768 1970-01-01 10:00 MACDATA
```

If I go into data its empty (its as if the drive has not mounted)
If I go into dvd its empty (its as if the drive has not mounted)

Thanks

Tim

P.S Loving 7.04

---

### Post by dcstar on 2007-04-24
> **higgins said:**
> Hi there

I previously had two volumes (mounting automatically at login) before the upgrade to feisty, /media/data (this is a SATA partition called MACDATA) AND /media/dvd, now they do not mount when I log in..
........

Install the **pysdm** package and that will allow you to see and mount whatever partitions it detects.

---

### Post by Zer0Nin3r on 2007-05-09
> **dcstar said:**
> Install the **pysdm** package and that will allow you to see and mount whatever partitions it detects.
Sweet!  Thank you so much, this is exactly what I was looking for.

---
